purpose: policy for extending current bounded PDG build scope by exactly one continuation step without losing boundedness
goals: preserve the existing bounded slice planner skeleton
allow exactly one more scope hop after an admitted bridge-completion file
improve current scope-limited two-hop continuation cases without turning into project-wide PDG
non_goals: solving nested multi-input propagation in this task alone
solving imported-result alias stitching in this task alone
final global budget optimization across every seed and family

current_planner_shape.stop_rule: planner currently stops after admitted bridge-completion files

proposed_extension.definition: a single extra file selected from an already-admitted bridge-completion anchor, following the same bridge family for exactly one more graph hop
proposed_extension.hard_stop: never recurse again from a bridge-continuation file

selection_policy.step_a: run the current bounded slice planner unchanged

anchor_requirements: candidate is not a module companion fallback
candidate is admitted, not only pruned

expansion_direction.callers: follow one incoming call-graph hop from the admitted completion anchor symbol
expansion_direction.callees: follow one outgoing call-graph hop from the admitted completion anchor symbol
expansion_direction.both: evaluate callers and callees independently, then union selected paths

candidate_filters: reject candidates reachable only through module-companion fallback

step_e.why_separate_from_tier2: continuation scope should not consume the existing tier2 bridge-completion budget, otherwise the one-step extension collapses back into current behavior

selection_policy.step_f: merge selected continuation paths into cache_update_paths, local_dfg_paths, and slice_selection_summary; then stop

minimum_internal_change: retain the admitted bridge-completion symbol id as an anchor
retain the admitted bridge-completion path as an anchor
carry bridge_kind from anchor into continuation selection metadata

budget_policy.bounded_invariant: maximum depth becomes root -> direct_boundary -> bridge_completion -> bridge_continuation, and never deeper

not_fixed_by_scope_alone.rust-nested-two-arg-summary-continuation: pair.rs can already be in scope; the remaining gap is propagation/summary mapping
not_fixed_by_scope_alone.rust-cross-file-wrapper-return-alias-cha: value.rs can already be in scope; the remaining gap is imported-result to alias to caller-result stitching
not_fixed_by_scope_alone.rust-three-boundary-bridge-budget-overfl: the remaining gap is tier2 budget policy, not missing depth after an admitted bridge-completion anchor

slice_selection_summary: selected files may now include bridge_continuation_file reasons
bridge_kind should be preserved from the admitted anchor family
via_symbol_id and via_path should still refer to the direct-boundary side
internal or public metadata should retain anchor symbol/path for debugging and future witness work
pruning_visibility: continuation candidates should be explainable when they lose to budget or stronger same-anchor siblings

planner_level: adds at most one continuation file per seed
does not derive continuation from module-companion fallback
preserves current tier2 behavior when no continuation-ready anchor exists
cli_level: rust two-hop wrapper-return case reports leaf.rs in slice_selection.summary
ruby two-hop require_relative case reports lib/leaf.rb in slice_selection.summary
non_regression: keep current direct-boundary success cases intact

implementation_entrypoint.preferred_location: extend src/bin/dimpact.rs::plan_bounded_slice rather than creating a brand-new planner
implementation_entrypoint.merge_strategy: run current selection first, then continuation expansion as a bounded post-pass
